entries.threat: Spoofed publisher GUID: attacker constructs a sample with a writer_guid matching a trusted publisher, causing the subscriber to accept data from an untrusted source
entries.mitigation: writer_guid is a 16-byte opaque token set by the local publisher (AtomicU64 counter + domain prefix); future RTPS transport must authenticate GUID origin via DDS-Security; tracked REQ-SEC-007, REQ-SEC-008

entries.threat: Topic name injection: caller passes a crafted topic string containing path separators or control characters, causing broker misrouting or log injection
entries.mitigation: Topic validated non-empty per REQ-SEC-001/REQ-SEC-013; exact HashMap key match prevents glob/wildcard expansion; future transport should add character allowlist

entries.threat: Payload flooding DoS: high-rate publisher writes arbitrarily large payloads, exhausting subscriber queue memory and degrading real-time performance
entries.mitigation: Back-pressure policy (DropNewest/DropOldest/Block) bounds queue depth per REQ-ASIL-008; payload size limit (REQ-SEC-002 PayloadTooLarge) deferred to RTPS transport milestone

entries.threat: Sample replay attack: attacker captures a valid relay::Message and replays it to inject stale or duplicate data into a safety-critical subscriber
entries.mitigation: Monotonic sequence numbers per REQ-ASIL-005 and REQ-SEC-007 allow duplicate detection; timestamp included in every Message; application layer must enforce freshness window; DDS-Security replay protection deferred to RTPS milestone

entries.threat: Cross-domain data exfiltration: participant on domain D subscribes to topics intended for domain D+1 due to missing access control
entries.mitigation: Each MockParticipant holds an isolated Broker; domains are fully partitioned per REQ-PART-001 and HAZ-004; future RTPS must enforce domain ID in UDP multicast group selection

entries.threat: Race condition on subscriber close: concurrent close() and recv() calls could read from a partially-freed SubInner, producing undefined output
entries.mitigation: SubInner uses AtomicBool + Notify for lock-free, race-free close signalling per REQ-CONC-001 and REQ-IEC-006; Mutex protects the sample queue; no unsafe code per REQ-ASIL-002

entries.threat: Deserialisation of external DDS payload causes memory safety violation or logic error in application layer
entries.mitigation: rust-DDS treats payloads as opaque Vec<u8>; no in-library deserialisation of application-level content; application owns decoding and must validate per REQ-SEC-004 and REQ-SEC-011

entries.threat: Unbounded publisher creation exhausts system resources: caller creates thousands of publishers without closing them, filling the Mutex-protected HashMap
entries.mitigation: Publisher lifecycle managed by caller; close() removes publisher state; future implementation should add per-participant publisher limit; tracked as resource exhaustion risk
